title: Documentation: Consolidate READMEs and Update Feature List
content:
## Overview

The project's documentation was overhauled in two commits: the main `README.md` was rewritten from a live codebase inventory, extra README files were deleted and their content merged in, and a `CLAUDE.md` project guidance file was added.

---

## README Consolidation

### Problem
The project had three README files (`README.md`, `README2.md`, `README3.md`). The main README was significantly out of date — features like the isometric 3D board, two-player mode, and most bots were either missing or still listed as "coming soon" despite being fully implemented. The extra files contained forward-looking ideas and user feedback with no permanent home.

### What Changed

**`README.md`** was rewritten top to bottom based on a full codebase inventory (keyboard bindings, game modes, bot profiles, rendering features, leaderboard mechanics, and UI elements were read directly from source files before any edits were made):
- Headline description updated to lead with the isometric 3D board.
- New **"Game modes"** section documents all four entry points: single player, two-player PvP, solo bot, and bot-vs-bot.
- **Bot strategy table** added covering all 11 bots.
- **Controls table** expanded with rotation, perspective toggle, overhead view, Enter, and Esc keys.
- **"Coming soon"** section trimmed to only genuinely unshipped items: color blind mode, first-person view, "snek" rename, and global/online leaderboards.

**`README2.md` and `README3.md`** were deleted. Their relevant content was merged into the "Coming soon" section of `README.md`:
- Color blind mode (appeared in both files — deduplicated to one entry).
- Possible rename to "snek".
- First-person view.
- Viewing other players' high scores (reframed as **global/online leaderboards** to distinguish from the local per-mode leaderboards that already exist).

Redundant section headers from the extra files ("future plans", "user feedback") were dropped rather than preserved as subsections, keeping `README.md` as a single source of truth.

---

## Roadmap Decisions

| Item | Status | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Isometric 3D board | ✅ Shipped — moved to feature list | Was listed as "coming soon"; fully implemented |
| Two-player mode | ✅ Shipped — moved to feature list | Was listed as "coming soon"; fully implemented |
| Color blind mode | 🔜 Roadmap | Unimplemented; retained from README2/3 |
| First-person view | 🔜 Roadmap | Unimplemented; retained from README2/3 |
| "Snek" rename | 🔜 Roadmap | Unimplemented; retained from README2/3 |
| Global leaderboards | 🔜 Roadmap | Re-added from deleted README3; distinct from existing local leaderboards |

---

## CLAUDE.md

`CLAUDE.md` was created at the repo root with a **"Use Jolli Memory"** section. It instructs Claude to:
- Recall prior branch context before planning or coding.
- Search across branches when design questions arise.
- Use the Jolli PR skill when opening pull requests.

This file was added on a separate branch (not bundled with any active feature branch) to keep the diff minimal and allow it to land independently. A project-level guidance file was chosen over a memory entry or hook because it is loaded into context every session and applies to all contributors — a hook would be too rigid for a "when relevant" guideline.

---

## Files Affected

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `README.md` | Rewritten |
| `README2.md` | Deleted |
| `README3.md` | Deleted |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Created |
